---
title: "06f-gene-expression.Rmd"
output: html_document
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
```{r}
# requiring the required code
require(tidyverse)
if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")

BiocManager::install("DESeq2")
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r}
# importing the dataset for MAC
require(tidyverse)
salmon.data <- read.delim("../../../Downloads/salmon.isoform.counts.matrix", header=TRUE , check.names = FALSE)
```
```{r}
# importing dataset for WINDOWS
require(tidyverse)
salmon.data <- read.delim("../../../../Downloads/salmon.isoform.counts.matrix", header=TRUE , check.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
colnames(salmon.data)[1] <- "ID"
head(salmon.data)
```

```{r}
head(salmon.data)
```

```{r}
#filtering for only 25 and 5 treatments
salmon.data$V1 = NULL
salmon.data$V2 = NULL
salmon.data$V3 = NULL
salmon.data$V4 = NULL
salmon.data$V5 = NULL
salmon.data$V6 = NULL
salmon.data$V7 = NULL
```
```{r}
#importing the other dataset on MAC
Sequencing.Picks <- read.csv("../metadata/metabolomics-sample-list.csv")
sequencing.picks.8.9.23 <- Sequencing.Picks %>% 
  filter(date == "8/9/23")
```
```{r}
#importing the other dataset on WINDOWS
Sequencing.Picks <- read.csv("../metadata/metabolomics-sample-list.csv")
sequencing.picks.8.9.23 <- Sequencing.Picks %>% 
  filter(date == "8/9/23")
```

```{r}
# making new data subset for end of exp THIS IS WHERE ROW NAMES GETS ADDED(?)
End.data <- salmon.data %>% 
  select(ID, any_of(sequencing.picks.8.9.23$crab.ID)) %>% 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round, 0))
```
```{r}
head(End.data)
```

```{r}
#importing final dataset
metadata.main <- read.csv("C:/Users/ashra/Downloads/metabolomics-sample-list.csv")
```
```{r}
# downloading deseq2
if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")

BiocManager::install("DESeq2")
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)
```
```{r}
# doing the tutorial
countsName <- "http://bioconnector.org/workshops/data/airway_scaledcounts.csv"
download.file(countsName, destfile = "airway_scaledcounts.csv", method = "auto")

countData <- read.csv('airway_scaledcounts.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ",")
head(countData)
```
```{r}
metaDataName <- "http://bioconnector.org/workshops/data/airway_metadata.csv"
download.file(metaDataName, destfile = "airway_metadata.csv", method = "auto")

metaData <- read.csv('airway_metadata.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ",")
metaData
```
```{r}
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData=countData, 
                              colData=metaData, 
                              design=~dex, tidy = TRUE)
```
```{r}
countsName <- "https://github.com/bioconnector/workshops/blob/master/data/airway_scaledcounts.csv"
download.file(countsName, destfile = "airway_scaledcounts.csv", method = "auto")

countData <- read.csv('airway_scaledcounts.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ",")
head(countData)
```
```{r}
countData <- read.csv("C:/Users/ashra/Downloads/airway_scaledcounts.csv", header = TRUE, sep = ",")
countData
```
```{r}
#getting the metadata now
metaData <- read.csv("../../../Downloads/ai", header = TRUE, sep = ",")
metaData
```
```{r}
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData=countData, 
                              colData=metaData, 
                              design=~dex, tidy = TRUE)
```
```{r}
dds <- DESeq(dds)
```
```{r}
?DESeq
```
```{r}
# looking at results table
res <- results(dds)
head(results(dds, tidy=TRUE))
```
```{r}
#summarize results
summary(res)
```
```{r}
res <- res[order(res$padj),]
head(res)
```
```{r}
par(mfrow=c(2,3))

plotCounts(dds, gene="ENSG00000152583", intgroup="dex")
plotCounts(dds, gene="ENSG00000179094", intgroup="dex")
plotCounts(dds, gene="ENSG00000116584", intgroup="dex")
plotCounts(dds, gene="ENSG00000189221", intgroup="dex")
plotCounts(dds, gene="ENSG00000120129", intgroup="dex")
plotCounts(dds, gene="ENSG00000148175", intgroup="dex")
```
```{r}
par(mfrow=c(1,1))
with(res, plot(log2FoldChange, -log10(pvalue), pch=20, main="Volcano plot", xlim=c(-3,3)))
with(subset(res, padj<.01 ), points(log2FoldChange, -log10(pvalue), pch=20, col="blue"))
with(subset(res, padj<.01 & abs(log2FoldChange)>2), points(log2FoldChange, -log10(pvalue), pch=20, col="red"))
```
```{r}
vsdata <- vst(dds, blind=FALSE)
plotPCA(vsdata, intgroup="dex")
```
```{r}
head(salmon.data)
```


```{r}
#doing the proper gene analysis
dds.crab <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData=End.data, 
                              colData=sequencing.picks.8.9.23, 
                              design=~treatment, tidy = TRUE)
```
```{r}
#trying to move column names
salmon.data$ID <- rownames(salmon.data)
End.data$ID <- rownames(End.data)
end.dat <- merge(End.data, salmon.data, by = "ID", all.x = TRUE)
```
```{r}
#priming the code so once error is fixed, it will take about 5 minutes to fix it
crab.stats <- DESeq(dds.crab)
```
```{r}
results.crab <- results(crab.stats)
head(results(crab.stats, tidy=TRUE))
results.main <- results.crab[order(results.crab$padj),]
head(results.main)
```
```{r}
par(mfrow=c(2,3))

plotCounts(crab.stats, gene="TRINITY_DN5483_c2_g1_i9", intgroup="row")
plotCounts(crab.stats, gene="TRINITY_DN3903_c0_g3_i1", intgroup="row")
plotCounts(crab.stats, gene="TRINITY_DN12380_c0_g1_i1", intgroup="row")
plotCounts(crab.stats, gene="TRINITY_DN7046_c0_g1_i3", intgroup="row")
plotCounts(crab.stats, gene="TRINITY_DN12380_c0_g1_i6", intgroup="row")
plotCounts(crab.stats, gene="TRINITY_DN178823_c0_g1_i1", intgroup="row")
#... continue from the graph I made upstairs - intgroup is messing me up
```
### moving onto the new data frame by trimming the old one
```{r}

```
